bottom_line: CR-00061 is a clean Phase-2 close. No agent thrash, no fix cycles. Addopts-merge pattern is proven muscle-memory; Phase-3 marker additions should follow the same replace-not-append rule. Recommended next Phase-3 CR: 3.2 (contract sweep + schemathesis).
coverage_notes: No raw run logs available (ai-dev/logs/ absent from worktree). Analysis based on S01-S03 reports (CR-00061, CR-00060, CR-00059) and ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md. S01, S02, S03 reports read in full. CR-00059 self-assess report read for Phase-2 cumulative cost reference.

findings.title: Phase-3 template should require smoke test capture for marker/addopts changes
findings.recommendation: Add a mandatory deliverable in Phase-3 template for any CR that adds a marker or modifies addopts: (a) a guard test asserting the new addopts form (single -m filter, correct filter chain), (b) a smoke test in S01 report showing a tagged test deselected in default run and collected under the new marker. Pattern demonstrated in CR-00061 S01 smoke test (CR-00061_S01_Backend_report.md lines 49-77).
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_S01_Backend_report.md:49-77 — smoke test demonstrating bidirectional marker deselection
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add smoke-test capture requirement to Phase-3 template for marker/addopts changes; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Cross-doc-square checklist item for prose rules in multiple surfaces
findings.recommendation: Phase-3 template should include a cross-doc-square checklist item (independent verification that a prose rule appears verbatim across all surfaces) whenever the CR modifies text in tests/CLAUDE.md, skill, and strategy doc simultaneously. CR-00061 S03 demonstrated this pattern effectively (CR-00061_S03_CodeReviewFinal_report.md lines 83-98). No automated grep check needed at this time — prose rule enforcement via reviewer checklist is sufficient.
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_S03_CodeReviewFinal_report.md:83-98 — cross-doc-square table showing all 5 rules present verbatim across 3 surfaces
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add cross-doc-square checklist item to Phase-3 template for prose rule changes; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Phase-3 template should include spike-then-setup for items with unknown cost
findings.recommendation: For Phase-3 items with unknown wall-clock cost (e.g. 3.4 cross-project isolation matrix may require two-project DB setup; 3.6 data-layer may have migration interaction unknowns), the Phase-3 template should mandate a spike-then-setup sequence (S01 spike measures cost, S02+ implements based on measured data). CR-00059 demonstrated this pattern effectively (measurement table in spike, then setup).
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00059/reports/CR-00059_S12_SelfAssess_report.md — Phase-2 recommendation: keep spike-then-setup for P2-CR-B and use audit-table deliverables for P2-CR-C
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add spike-then-setup requirement to Phase-3 template for high-uncertainty items; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Phase-3 template should require fabricated-fixture verification for scripts parsing structured output
findings.recommendation: When a Phase-3 CR adds a script that parses structured output (pytest logs, CLI output, etc.), require fabricated-input verification as a deliverable: hand-crafted input matching expected format → expected output → verify script exits with correct code. Not mocked — real inputs. CR-00061's aggregator test (CR-00061_S01_Backend_report.md lines 81-119) and S03's SKIPPED-row verification (CR-00061_S03_CodeReviewFinal_report.md lines 152-164) demonstrate the pattern.
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_S01_Backend_report.md:81-119 — aggregator fabricated-log verification (pass-fail-pass → exit 1, all-passing → exit 0)
ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_S03_CodeReviewFinal_report.md:152-164 — S03 independent SKIPPED-row re-verification (test_delete SKIPPED not flagged as flake)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add fabricated-fixture verification requirement to Phase-3 template for scripts parsing structured output; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Phase-3 template should include marker auto-apply hook pattern for auto-declaring markers
findings.recommendation: When Phase-3 adds a marker that should auto-apply to a subset of tests (e.g. 3.4 tenant_a/tenant_b isolation markers; 3.5 negative security markers), require the conftest pytest_collection_modifyitems approach demonstrated in CR-00060 (tests/unit/properties/conftest.py lines — marker auto-applied to properties tests via hook). This is cleaner than requiring every test to self-declare and ensures consistent coverage.
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00060/reports/CR-00060_S01_Backend_report.md — conftest.py registers ci/dev/deep profiles + pytest_collection_modifyitems auto-apply hook; pytest --collect-only -m properties shows 19 collected, pytest --collect-only -m 'not properties' shows 0 in properties dir
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Document marker auto-apply hook pattern in Phase-3 template; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Phase-3 template should include measurement table as standard deliverable for non-trivial runtime items
findings.recommendation: Any Phase-3 CR with a non-trivial runtime cost (mutation scope decisions, E2E full-suite runs, property test profiling) should produce a measurement table as a standard deliverable in S01: metric | before | after | delta. CR-00059's spike measurement table (ai-dev/active/CR-00059/evidences/pre/cr-00059-spike-measurements.txt) is the canonical reference.
evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00059/reports/CR-00059_S12_SelfAssess_report.md — primary process finding: mutation runs preempted by coverage fail-under before mutant execution; spike measurement table informed P2-CR-A-followup
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add measurement table requirement to Phase-3 template for non-trivial runtime items; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/templates/Phase-3-CR-Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Phase-3 sequencing: start with 3.2 (contract sweep + schemathesis) before 3.1 (E2E)
findings.recommendation: Phase-3 should start with item 3.2 (contract/no-5xx route sweep + schemathesis) rather than 3.1 (E2E layer). Item 3.2 is more tractable (single project, existing OpenAPI spec, clear ACs), produces faster first deliverable, and generates learnings about schemathesis patterns that transfer to 3.1. Item 3.1 is the largest Phase-3 item and should be second, when the team has more bandwidth and the E2E runner pattern (playwright-cli) is better understood from the quarantine workflow's on-demand model.
evidence: ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md §7 — Phase-3 items 3.1-3.6 listed in order; 3.2 is contract sweep + schemathesis
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Start Phase-3 with item 3.2 (contract sweep + schemathesis) as the first CR; analyzed in CR-00061 self-assess (ai-dev/active/CR-00061/reports/CR-00061_self_assess_report.md). Target: ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md. Effort: S.

marker_deselection_foolproofness.verdict: PASS — smoke test worked first try, no friction
marker_deselection_foolproofness.lesson: addopts-merge pattern (replace, don't append) is proven. Phase-3 marker additions (3.4 tenant markers, 3.5 negative marker) should follow same rule. Guard test test_addopts_deselects_quarantine permanently encodes the constraint.

aggregator_skipped_row_handling.verdict: PASS — no false positive on SKIPPED rows
aggregator_skipped_row_handling.evidence: CR-00061_S03_CodeReviewFinal_report.md lines 152-164: test_delete (all SKIPPED) not flagged as flake; test_update (PASSED→FAILED→PASSED) correctly identified

file_an_incident_rule_enforceability.verdict: PASS — prose rule enforced via reviewer diligence; no automated check needed
file_an_incident_rule_enforceability.evidence: S02 checklist item 7 + S03 cross-doc-square independently verified all 5 rules verbatim across 3 surfaces

cumulative_phase_2_cost.cr_00060: ~2.5 h (5 property modules + verification + docs + QV gates)
cumulative_phase_2_cost.cr_00061: ~2.0 h (workflow + aggregator + smoke test + QV gates)
cumulative_phase_2_cost.phase_1_comparison: Phase 1: 5 CRs over 5 days (~15 h). Phase 2: 3 CRs in 1 day (~7.5 h). Phase-2 per-CR average ~2.5 h.
cumulative_phase_2_cost.phase_3_cost_cap_recommendation: 2.5-3.0 h per Phase-3 CR unless spike component

rank_1.value: HIGHEST immediate value — smoke test demonstrated addopts mechanics working; file-an-incident rule immediately actionable for all developers
rank_1.recommendation: Expand: wire test-flake-detect into nightly cron (not yet done)

rank_2.value: HIGH infrastructure value — ci profile runs in ~1.5s as part of make test-unit; latent value when regressions occur in covered modules
rank_2.recommendation: Consider widening scope to more modules guided by P2-CR-A-followup-mutation-block findings

rank_3.value: MEDIUM infrastructure value — 0 mutants generated (blocked by coverage fail-under); actual score signal pending P2-CR-A-followup-mutation-block
rank_3.recommendation: P2-CR-A-followup-mutation-block is required to unlock value

phase_3_sequencing_recommendation.reasoning: Most tractable Phase-3 entry point (single project, existing OpenAPI spec, clear ACs). Produces faster first deliverable than 3.1. Schema-first pattern shares DNA with Hypothesis (CR-00060), familiar to team. 3.1 (E2E) is largest Phase-3 item — second CR when bandwidth + learnings from 3.2 are available. 3.4 (cross-project) requires two registered projects before it can be meaningfully exercised.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: A — Smoke test capture in S01 report for marker/addopts changes
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required for every Phase-3 CR with marker change or addopts change. Must show tagged test deselected in default run AND collected under new marker. Must be reverted before CR completion.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: B — Measurement table as deliverable for non-trivial runtime items
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required for any Phase-3 CR with non-trivial wall-clock cost. Format: metric | before | after | delta. Captured in S01 report.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: C — Cross-doc-square checklist item for prose rules in multiple surfaces
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required when CR modifies prose rules appearing in tests/CLAUDE.md, skill, and strategy doc simultaneously.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: D — Spike-then-setup for items with unknown cost
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required for Phase-3 items with unknown wall-clock (e.g. 3.4 cross-project isolation, 3.6 data-layer). S01 spike measures; S02+ implements.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: E — Fabricated-fixture verification for scripts parsing structured output
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required when CR adds a script parsing pytest logs, CLI output, etc. Hand-crafted inputs → expected output → verify correct exit code. Not mocked.

patterns_to_bake.pattern: F — Marker auto-apply hook via conftest pytest_collection_modifyitems
patterns_to_bake.template_recommendation: Required when Phase-3 adds a marker that auto-applies to a subset. Conftest approach cleaner than per-test self-declaration.

open_followups.description: Widen mutmut scope from orch/daemon/ to all of orch/; flip from non-blocking to blocking PR gate after burn-in. Cost/speed unknown until CR-00059 spike numbers analyzed.
Scrub the 621-entry assertion baseline (543 tautology / 71 no-assert / 7 mock-only). Low urgency, incremental, chunkable by module.
